name_full: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Organizations designing developing deploying or governing generative AI systems
why_it_matters: Provides a cross-sector profile and companion resource for applying AI RMF 1.0 to generative AI risks
key_outputs: Generative AI profile for AI RMF 1.0
notes: Published July 2024 as NIST Trustworthy and Responsible AI 600-1

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: AI developers deployers users evaluators and governance teams
why_it_matters: Provides a voluntary framework for mapping measuring managing and governing trustworthy AI risks
notes: Released January 2023 through NIST consensus-driven public process

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Federal agencies and vendors using cryptographic modules
why_it_matters: Defines the mandatory US federal security requirements and assurance levels for cryptographic modules
notes: Final FIPS 140-3 published March 2019 and supersedes FIPS 140-2

name_full: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Federal contractors nonfederal organizations and CUI system operators
why_it_matters: Defines recommended security requirements for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in nonfederal systems
notes: Final SP 800-171 Rev. 3 published May 2024 and supersedes Rev. 2

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Federal agencies risk managers and security practitioners
why_it_matters: Provides guidance for conducting risk assessments as part of organizational risk management

name_full: Risk Management Framework for Information Systems and Organizations
issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Federal agencies system owners authorizing officials and security teams
why_it_matters: Defines the Risk Management Framework process for categorizing selecting implementing assessing authorizing and monitoring controls

name_full: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Federal agencies cloud providers auditors and cybersecurity programs
why_it_matters: Provides the core catalog of security and privacy controls used across US federal systems FedRAMP and many regulatory mappings
notes: Current Rev. 5 page includes updates and OSCAL supplemental material

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sector_applicability: Government digital service providers identity systems and relying parties
why_it_matters: Defines identity proofing authentication federation and related technical requirements for digital services
notes: Final SP 800-63-4 published July 2025 and supersedes SP 800-63-3
